div1.entryFree:
Score, vb. 1) to notch, to cut, to chip: let us s. their backs, Ant. IV, 7, 12.
2) to mark or set down for an account; absol.: after he --s, he never pays the s. All's IV, 3, 253. here's no --ing but upon the pate, H4A V, 3, 31. trans.: nor need I tallies thy dear love to s. Sonn. 122, 10. she will s. your fault upon my pate, Err. I, 2, 65. s. me up for the lyingest knave in Christendom, Shr. Ind. 2, 25. s. a pint of bastard, H4A II, 4, 29. have you --d me? Oth. IV, 1, 130 (i. e. made my reckoning. Q1 stored).
